project_title.value: Dennis Color of Title-Park City Land Sale

value: Bureau of Land Management, Salt Lake Field Office
Salt Lake Meridian, Summit County, Utah, Township 2 South, Range 4 East, Section 15, lots 29 and 30.

**Project Description: The Bureau of Land Management (BLM) has received an application to purchase 0.78 acres of land in Summit County, Utah, under the Color of Title Act of December 22, 1928, as amended. Sufficient evidence has been presented to verify that the claimant of the public lands has held the property in good faith and peaceful, adverse possession for a minimum of 20 years under Class 1 claim of Color of Title, and has placed valuable improvements on the property located in Park City, Utah. Once the BLM determines the claim to be valid and the required reports, appraisal, survey, and public notification are completed, the United States is required under the Act to sell the property to the claimant for fair market value, less the equities valuation. The Federal subsurface mineral estate would be reserved to the United States. No environmental analysis or Section 106 consultation is required because once the color of title claim is determined to be valid, transfer of the land is a mandatory action and the conviction is that any contamination, historic properties, or improvements are private property. Transfer stipulations would be applied consistent with 43 Code of Federal Regulations (CFR) 2541.5, wherein the applicant would be required to publish once a week for four consecutive weeks, at his expense, in a designated newspaper, a notice allowing all persons claiming the land adversely to file in the office specified in 43 CFR 2541.1-2(a) their objections to the issuance of patent under the application. Furthermore, required under 43 CFR 2541.4(b) the applicant would be required to make payment of the sale price of the land within the time stated in the request for payment. Other protective measures would not be warranted.**
Department of the Interior - Bureau of Land Management

project_title.value: Commnet Communications Use Lease at the Hill 71 Communications Site

value: NMPM, T. 43 N., R. 4 W., sec. 35, lots 6 and 7, Colorado

DESCRIPTION OF PROPOSED ACTION: The proposed action is to issue a communication use lease (which would include a right-of-way for the use and maintenance of the Sawmill Park Road) to the applicant. The Sawmill Park Road (BLM Road 3322) provides the primary access to the site. The lease would be for the installation and maintenance of the communication facilities. The Hill 71 Communications Site Management Plan would be made part of the lease and the lease would be managed according to the Plan.

The applicant's bid proposal describes the details of how the facilities would be constructed and would be incorporated into the lease (see Exhibit C). The following facilities would be constructed/installed at the Hill 71 communications site just east of the existing facilities at the site:
* Four 10' x 16' prefabricated equipment shelters, each placed on 14' x 20' concrete slab
* A 120-foot tall self-supporting tower, 17' x 17' at the base
* A back-up generator placed on a 6' x 14' concrete pad
* Three 1,000-gallon propane tanks, each approximately 4' x 16', placed on interlock
pavers
* A 7-foot tall chain link fence around the new facilities, an area approximately 78' x 100'
* Buried electric lines from the Gunnison County Electric Association, Inc. (GCEA) power
source, along the Hill 71 access road, to a multi-bank utility box within the fenced area,
then to each shelter and the tower.
Construction is planned for summer of 2021, to be completed by the fall of 2021.

If there is a need based on customer demand, up to two additional shelters could be installed in
the future. They would be located within the fenced area. Also, if there is a future need, a 30-feet
extension to the tower could be added.

Each shelter can accommodate one or two users, depending on the space needed for tenants'
equipment. In accordance with the Hill 71 Communications Site Management Plan, all current
and any future commercial users would be required to locate within this facility, as long as space
and compatibility allows. The existing authorized facilities (BLM tower, Hinsdale County
building, back-up generator) would house current and any future federal, State, County, and/or
municipal government users/uses.

Stipulations that direct management of the communication use lease would be included in the
lease (see Exhibit B). The Stipulations address maintenance, noxious weed control, and
protection of cultural resources, among other things.
Department of the Interior - Bureau of Land Management

project_title.value: Hell's Gulch Communication Tower Renewal Permit

value: Conventional Energy Production - Land-based Oil & Gas
Land Use or Forest Management Plan

Decision
I have decided to implement the request by Laramie Energy LLC (Laramie) to apply for a new permit that would renew the use and occupancy authorized by Special Use Permit RIF184. Special Use Permit RIF184 will expire at midnight on December 31, 2020.

RIF184 authorizes the operation and maintenance of a free-standing communication tower approximately 40 feet tall, including the antenna at the top of the tower, galvanized fencing on a 100 sq. ft. footprint and associated equipment utilized for the transfer of production data from the company's area natural gas wells. The communication tower is located on the Hells Gulch 26-11 gas well pad (now reclaimed). The new permit will also include approximately 6,864 lineal feet of road to access road the tower location.

Laramie has also requested authorization to move the existing tower approximately 75 feet to the south and maintain access to the tower area via Forest Service Road (FSR) 815 (0.9 miles) and FSR 815.1A (0.4 miles). There will be no new disturbance on the access roads or at the adjusted tower platform. Public motorized access to the area will not be authorized and the roads will be authorized for motorized use by the permit holder or other authorized users only.

As of the date of this decision the well pad has been fully recontoured except for the area retained for placement and use of the communication tower, including room to turn a vehicle around during maintenance activities. The associated equipment consists of an antenna, 2 solar panels, electronic equipment, and fencing (no more than 10' x 10' square and 5' high). All surfaces that can be painted without degrading the communication function shall be painted Federal Color 34095.

The following access restrictions will apply to the permit per previous NEPA actions:
* Travel Management Update:
    * The access gate located on NFSR 816 must remain closed and locked at all times during the period of November 22 through May 21 of each year.
    * The access gate located on NFSR 815 must remain closed and locked at all times.
    * The closures meet the White River National Forest Travel Management Plan for the area.
* Big Game Winter Range Timing Limitation – Condition of Approval: To reduce impacts to wintering big game, daily operation and maintenance activities at the communication site shall be conducted between 9 a.m. and 3 p.m. during the Timing Limitation (TL) period from December 1 through April 30 annually.

A reclamation bond in the amount of $80,685 will be retained for the life of the communication tower. If Laramie or any subsequent permit holder determines that the tower is no longer needed the access roads and tower pad will be fully reclaimed as determined in the Plains Exploration and Production Company Hells Gulch North, Phase 2 Environmental Analysis (2008).

Laramie or any subsequent permit holder will maintain FSR815 and FSR 815.1A as outlined in an Operations and Maintenance Plan. Additionally, a performance or surety bond in an amount to be determined will be required.

This decision will be implemented through issuance of a special use authorization that meets the requirements of the decision and Forest Service regulations. Forest Service regulations require the applicant to: submit an operation and maintenance plan that complies with the decision requirements; pay any necessary fees; post any necessary bonds; and secure any state or federal permits or authorizations required by law.

Background
The communication tower is located on the 26-11 well pad located in Section 26, T8S, R92W of the 6th PM, Mesa County, Colorado. As of November 5, 2020, the well pad has been reclaimed except for a small cul-de-sac with room to access and perform maintenance on the tower. To allow for efficient reclamation of the pad the tower has been moved from the original location to the end of FSR 815.1A. FSR 815 and FSR 815.1A were constructed to access the well pad and will be used to access the communication tower for the life of the permit.

The area is located within critical winter range for deer and elk. Industry activities and wildlife have commonly used this area for some time, and both seem to be able to meet their need when seasonal wildlife restrictions are followed. Other wildlife conflicts and cultural concerns were not evident.

Purpose and Need
The communication tower overlooks the entire basin where Laramie operates multiple gas wells. Due to the mountainous terrain, this location is the only developed location that can adequately provide communication service to Laramie's area wells and locations. The operation and maintenance of this communication tower allows for the remote transfer of production data related to Laramie area wells, including loss of pressure in well casings resulting in emergency shutdowns. The public will benefit from the continued reduction of vehicle traffic related to the production data captured. Without this tower Laramie would be required to make daily trips to each location.
